name: Upside-Down Apple Tart
recipeIngredient: 3 tbsp Margarine (cut into small pieces and chilled)
recipeInstructions:
Filling:
5 Medium Golden Delicious apples, peeled and cut into 8 wedges
1 Tablespoon Lemon juice
Sauce:
2 Tablespoons Brown sugar
1/4 Cup Plus 2 Tablespoons Vanilla low-fat yogurt
1 Tablespoon Low-fat sour cream
Crust:
Combine flour and 2 tablespoons sugar in a bowl; cut in margarine with a pastry blender until the mixture resembles coarse meal. Sprinkle ice water, 1 tablespoon at a time, over the surface; toss with a fork until dry ingredients are moistened and the mixture is crumbly. (Do not form a ball) Press the mixture into a 4-inch circle on heavy-duty plastic wrap, and chill for 15 minutes. Roll dough, still covered, to an 11-inch circle. Place in freezer for 10 minutes or until the plastic wrap can be easily removed.
Filling:
Combine apples, 1 tablespoon sugar, and lemon juice in a bowl; toss well. Let stand for 15 minutes. Wrap the handle of a 10-inch heavy skillet with aluminum foil. Sprinkle remaining 1/2 cup sugar in the skillet, and place over medium heat. Caramelize by stirring often until sugar melts and is golden (about 6 minutes). Add apple mixture, and cook 5 minutes or just until apples are tender, stirring constantly. Remove from heat. Remove plastic wrap from dough. Place dough on top of apples in skillet, gently tucking dough around the edge of the skillet. Cut slits in dough for steam to escape.
Bake at 425 degrees for 20 minutes or until browned. Invert onto a serving platter.
Sauce:
Combine brown sugar, yogurt, and sour cream; stir well. Drizzle over warm tart.
